task_completion.description: GIMP is open with a white canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible with a white image canvas open.
The Pencil Tool icon is highlighted or selected in the GIMP toolbox.

task_completion.description: The foreground color is set to red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The foreground color swatch in the GIMP toolbox shows bright red.

task_completion.description: A red line is drawn on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There is a distinct red mark or line on the white canvas.
The red line is perfectly straight, indicating the use of the Shift+click method rather than freehand drawing, and is at least roughly 100 pixels long.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to draw the line.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with GIMP tools and the canvas.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to draw the line.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show commands (like ImageMagick) that directly modify the image file to add a line.

integrity.description: The line is drawn on the existing blank canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the blank canvas first, followed by the appearance of the line, rather than opening a new pre-drawn image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires drawing a straight red line on a 600x400 white canvas in GIMP. The intended method is using the Pencil Tool and setting the foreground color to bright red (RGB: 255, 0, 0). A straight line is typically drawn by clicking one point, holding Shift, and clicking another point. The final line must be red, straight, and have a minimum length of 100 pixels.
